text:
There once was a wise old turkey named Harold. Harold had traveled far and wide, learning many lessons about life and friendship.

One day, a little kitten named Fluff came traipsing into Harold's Thanksgiving feast. Fluff was timid but friendly. Harold didn't talk much, but he seemed to approve of Fluff.

Fluff stayed at Harold's house for weeks. They played together under the big oak tree. They watched the clouds drift by. They talked about dreams - Fluff's dreams about mice, and Harold about the stars.

One afternoon, while looking at the stars, Harold smiled knowingly.

"You've got bright dreams, Fluff," he said gently.

Fluff blinked, confused. "Me?" she squeaked.

"I mean it. Stars often hold secret dreams. And you've got a big heart."

Harold watched as Fluff nestled down to sleep at his feet. He petted her gently. In his own way, Harold whispered secrets to the stars. Tales
